title: Proposal
text: GLP-1 receptor signaling may regulate downstream genes involved in the cAMP/PKA pathway. In this project, we analyze publicly available data to evaluate whether tissues with higher GLP-1R expression show greater downstream pathway gene activity and whether treatment is associated with increased expression of GLP-1R and its downstream signalling genes compared with untreated samples.

title: Proposal
text: GLP-1 receptor signaling may regulate downstream genes involved in the cAMP/PKA pathway. In this project, we analyze publicly available data to evaluate whether tissues with higher GLP-1R expression show greater downstream pathway gene activity and whether treatment is associated with increased expression of GLP-1R and its downstream signalling genes compared with untreated samples.

title: Proposal
text:
2 Dataset(s)

Source: NCBI GEO Public Dataset (GSE293291)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE293291
Sample size: 60 samples
Key variables:

Tissue type
Genotype
Treatment type
Gene expression measurements

License/usage: The dataset is publicly available through GEO and includes downloadable processed expression files as well as raw data in SRA. The GEO accession page does not list a separate explicit reuse license, so we cite the GEO accession page when using the data.

title: Proposal
text:
3 Planned analysis
Describe your plan in bullets:

Data cleaning and preprocessing

Combine expression files into one dataset
Match metadata to expression matrix (GSM IDs)
Log2 transformation and filtering of low-expression genes
Remove zero-variance genes before analysis

Cleaning/QC

Perform PCA to identify clustering and outliers
Evaluate variance explained by principal components
Check separation by treatment and genotype

Visualizations

PCA plots for QC
Volcano plots for differential expression
Heatmaps of top differentially expressed genes

Statistical/ML approach

Build design matrix based on genotype, treatment, and tissues
Fit linear model using limma
Identify differentially expressed genes

Validation & Intrepretation

Compare treated vs untreated groups

Peptide Treatment: mGIPR-Ab/P1

Vehicle Treatment

Focus on balanced comaprisons
Interpret results in context of GLP-1R and cAMP/PKA pathway.

title: Proposal
text:
4 Deliverables

Website sections
Key figures/tables

PCA Plot for QC
Volcano Plot
Heatmaps
Differential Expression Results Table

Any optional stretch goals

title: GLP-1R Expression Predicts Activation of cAMP/PKA Signaling Pathways
text: This project looks at how GLP-1 receptor (GLP1R) expression relates to downstream signaling genes, especially in the cAMP/PKA pathway. We are using a bulk RNA-seq dataset from mice that includes different genotypes, treatments (vehicle vs drug), and tissues (liver and iWAT) to compare gene expression patterns. Our approach involves cleaning and filtering the data, running PCA for quality control, and using the Limma package to perform differential expression analysis across groups . We are specifically testing whether treated samples and GLP1R-expressing samples show higher expression of downstream pathway genes. The main deliverables will include PCA plots, volcano plots, heatmaps, and a table of differentially expressed genes. These results will help us understand how GLP-1 signaling affects gene expression across tissues.

This project looks at how GLP-1 receptor (GLP1R) expression relates to downstream signaling genes, especially in the cAMP/PKA pathway. We are using a bulk RNA-seq dataset from mice that includes different genotypes, treatments (vehicle vs drug), and tissues (liver and iWAT) to compare gene expression patterns . Our approach involves cleaning and filtering the data, running PCA for quality control, and using the Limma package to perform differential expression analysis across groups . We are specifically testing whether treated samples and GLP1R-expressing samples show higher expression of downstream pathway genes. The main deliverables will include PCA plots, volcano plots, heatmaps, and a table of differentially expressed genes. These results will help us understand how GLP-1 signaling affects gene expression across tissues.
